label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out how many 3-digit numbers have digits that add up to exactly 7. Let me start by understanding the problem. First, a 3-digit number ranges from 100 to 999. That means the first digit (hundreds place) can't be zero; it has to be between 1 and 9. The other two digits (tens and ones places) can be between 0 and 9. The sum of all three digits should be exactly 7. Hmm, this seems like a problem where I can use combinations with some constraints. The general idea is to find the number of non-negative integer solutions to the equation: a + b + c = 7, where a is the hundreds digit (1-9), and b and c are the tens and ones digits
